    have h‚āĀ : (0 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ąą convexHull ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě (Set.range fun m : ‚Ąē => X (0 + m)) := by
      -- Since convexHull contains the zero vector in this context
      apply subset_convexHull
      use 0
      <;> simp
      <;> aesop
    intro n
    -- Use the fact that 0 is in the convex hull for n = 0 and extend to all n
    have h‚āā : (0 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ąą convexHull ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě (Set.range fun m : ‚Ąē => X (n + m)) := by

    filter_upwards [ae_restrict_mem measurableSet_univ] with ŌČ
    simp [tendsto_const_nhds]

have h_main : ‚ąÉ (Y : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) (Y_lim : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě), (‚ąÄ n, Y n ‚ąą convexHull ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě (Set.range fun m ‚Ü¶ X (n + m))) ‚ąß Measurable Y_lim ‚ąß ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāP, Tendsto (Y ¬∑ ŌČ) atTop (ūĚďĚ (Y_lim ŌČ)) := by
    use fun n ŌČ => (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ŌČ, fun ŌČ => (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ŌČ
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro n
      -- Prove that Y n is in the convex hull of the range of X
      have h‚āĀ : (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ‚ü®‚ü© ‚ąą convexHull ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě (Set.range fun m : ‚Ąē => X (n + m)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the convex hull contains the set itself
        have h‚āā : (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ‚ü®‚ü© ‚ąą Set.range fun m : ‚Ąē => X (n + m) := by
          -- Choose m = 0 to show that the element is in the range
          use 0
          <;> simp_all [hX]
          <;>
          aesop
        -- Since the convex hull contains the set, it contains (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ‚ü®‚ü©
        exact subset_convexHull h‚āā
      -- Simplify the expression to match the form we need
      simpa using h‚āĀ
    ¬∑ constructor
      ¬∑ -- Prove that Y_lim is measurable
        have h‚āā : Measurable (fun ŌČ : ő© => (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ŌČ) := by
          -- Use the fact that X 0 is measurable and scalar multiplication is measurable
          have h‚āÉ : Measurable (X 0) := hX 0
          exact by
            -- Prove that scalar multiplication is measurable
            apply Measurable.smul_const
            exact h‚āÉ
        exact h‚āā
      ¬∑ -- Prove that Y ŌČ tends to Y_lim ŌČ for almost every ŌČ
        have h‚āÉ : ‚ąÄŠĶź (ŌČ : ő©) ‚ąāP, Tendsto (fun x : ‚Ąē => (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ŌČ) atTop (ūĚďĚ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ŌČ)) := by
          -- Since the sequence is constant, it trivially converges to itself
          filter_upwards [P.ae.univ] with ŌČ
          have h‚āĄ : Tendsto (fun x : ‚Ąē => (1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ŌČ) atTop (ūĚďĚ ((1 : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě) ‚ÄĘ X 0 ŌČ)) := by
            -- Use the fact that the sequence is constant
            apply tendsto_const_nhds.congr'
            filter_upwards [Filter.eventually_ge_atTop 0] with n hn
            <;> simp [hn]
            <;>
            aesop
          exact h‚āĄ
        -- Simplify the expression to match the form we need
        simpa using h‚āÉ
  -- Use the main result to complete the proof
  exact h_main

some `i` (because otherwise we would have all `t_i = 0`, which contradicts `‚ąĎ t_i = 1`). 

This means that we can only get `Y n = 0` if there exists `m` such that `X (n + m) = 0`. 

But if `X (n + m) > 0` for all `m`, then we cannot get `Y n = 0`.
